title: Handle :ok and {:error, reason} return values
prompt: Write a worker `MyApp.Workers.FetchUserDataWorker` (queue `:external_api`, max_attempts 5) whose perform/1 accepts `%{"user_id" => id}` and calls `MyApp.ExternalAPI.fetch(id)`. On `{:ok, data}` it calls `MyApp.Users.store(id, data)` and returns `:ok`. On `{:error, :not_found}` it should `{:cancel, :user_gone}`. On any other `{:error, reason}` it returns `{:error, reason}`. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/fetch_user_data_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Canonical worker with three return branches — tests each symbol of the return-value protocol individually.
